name: ExtensionImplementationGuide_FhirVersion
title: R5: fhirVersion
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion` 1..* `code`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion` 1..* `code`
*  R4B: `ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion` 1..* `code`
*  R4: `ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion` 1..* `code`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.comment:
Element `ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
Most implementation guides target a single version - e.g. they describe how to use a particular version, and the profiles and examples etc. are valid for that version. But some implementation guides describe how to use multiple different versions of FHIR to solve the same problem, or in concert with each other. Typically, the requirement to support multiple versions arises as implementation matures and different implementation communities are stuck at different versions by regulation or market dynamics.

element.short: FHIR Version(s) this Implementation Guide targets
element.definition: The version(s) of the FHIR specification that this ImplementationGuide targets - e.g. describes how to use. The value of this element is the formal version of the specification, without the revision number, e.g. [publication].[major].[minor], which is 4.6.0. for this version.
element.comment: Most implementation guides target a single version - e.g. they describe how to use a particular version, and the profiles and examples etc. are valid for that version. But some implementation guides describe how to use multiple different versions of FHIR to solve the same problem, or in concert with each other.
